user_content:
Damian, here is an example of a application I wrote, please remember all of the details about me and my (Drongo's) writing style:
 Functional neural interfaces (FNIs) is a rapidly expanding, multi-disciplinary field that could potentially revolutionize neuroscience. However, many current FNIs are highly invasive, not feasible in humans, or can only record from, at most, one region of the brain. My research focuses on nano functional neural interfaces (nFNIs) to solve the stated problems. My penultimate goal is to design and safely implement a system that allows for high-resolution, bidirectional communication with the nervous system while being minimally invasive. I’ve been enthralled with creating this system since I was in elementary school. It is a project that will need many interdisciplinary technological advances to achieve, but I believe that such a system would have many extraordinary impacts on both research and society. 
 While I am specifically interested in researching nFNIs, I wanted to ensure that I had a wide range of research experience in neuroscience to better myself as a researcher. I had the honor of taking part in the 2020 summer undergraduate research program in Computational Neuroscience hosted by Carnegie Mellon University and the University of Pittsburgh. Due to the coronavirus, the internship was converted to an online format, which required me to work mostly independently. I worked in Dr. Aaron Batista’s lab under the guidance of his graduate student Erinn Grigsby where another student and I studied “Decoder Performance Classif …[truncated]

I'm trying to plan a gift for my niece's birthday, which is coming up soon. Can you give me some ideas for a nice gift in the $200 range? By the way, I just got a great gift for my sister recently - a silver necklace with a small pendant from Tiffany's that cost around $200.

I think I'll check out Alex and Ani for their customization options. I've heard great things about their brand and I like that they have a wide range of designs to choose from. By the way, I recently got my sister a silver necklace with a small pendant from Tiffany's that cost around $200, and she loved it.

CHAPTER NINE


COMMUNITY BUILDING & MANAGEMENT

What Are Online Communities?
Building an online community is becoming an increasingly important component of digital
marketing strategies, regardless of the size of your company or the scope of your brand's reach.
In the real world, communities serve as the bedrock upon which contemporary society is
built. They o er a support system and facilitate the exchange of information. We humans
are social creatures by nature, and we have a tendency to feel more secure when we are
participating in activities that involve other people. When things get di cult, communities
step up to o er assistance, advice, and rst-hand experience, and as a result, a strong
bond is formed between individuals from a wide variety of walks of life. It is important not
to undervalue the in uence that can be exerted by a group when its members unite
around a common purpose or interest and work toward a common goal.
Empowering groups of people to collaborate and communicate online are online
communities such as Facebook groups, forums, and hashtags. Online communities could
be the secret to your success on the internet, whether it's through connecting with others
over a common interest or following a well-known brand on social media. You can
position your business and brand as an industry leader by tapping into online
communities or by forming your own digital group. This will allow you to create a section
of the internet where your brand provides information, advice, support, tips, resolutions,
answers, and entertainment. Within this minia …[truncated]

Classify the below papers into 10 appropriate categories

Papers: 

MedMCQA: A Large-scale Multi-Subject Multi-Choice Dataset for Medical domain Question Answering https://proceedings.mlr.press/v174/pal22a.html

Medical Exam Question Answering with Large-scale Reading Comprehension https://arxiv.org/pdf/1802.10279.pdf

PubMedQA: A Dataset for Biomedical Research Question Answering https://www.aclweb.org/anthology/D19-1259.pdf

emrQA: A Large Corpus for Question Answering on Electronic Medical Records https://www.aclweb.org/anthology/D18-1258.pdf

QASC: A Dataset for Question Answering via Sentence Composition https://arxiv.org/pdf/1910.11473.pdf

MMM: Multi-stage Multi-task Learning for Multi-choice Reading Comprehension https://arxiv.org/pdf/1910.00458.pdf

SOCIAL IQA: Commonsense Reasoning about Social Interactions https://arxiv.org/pdf/1904.09728.pdf

Improving Question Answering with External Knowledge https://arxiv.org/pdf/1902.00993.pdf

Question Answering as Global Reasoning over Semantic Abstractions https://arxiv.org/pdf/1906.03672.pdf

Automatic Question Answering for Medical MCQs: Can It Go Further than Information Retrieval? https://www.aclweb.org/anthology/R19-1049.pdf

Overview of the MEDIQA 2019 Shared Task on Textual Inference, Question Entailment and Question Answering https://www.aclweb.org/anthology/W19-5039.pdf

HEAD-QA: A Healthcare Dataset for Complex Reasoning https://www.aclweb.org/anthology/P19-1092.pdf

Improving Retrieval-Based Question Answering with Deep Inference Models https://arxiv.org/pdf/1812.02971.pdf

Explain Yourself! …[truncated]

An SDR gave the following answers: "What do you like best about Wingman by Clari? I love that the recordings and call feedback are available immediately af ter the call. This allows me to look back and ensure that I' ve covered all actionable commitments that I made during the conversation. This really allows me to be present during the call and not distracted by notetaking . What do you dislike about Wingman by Clari? There is a bit of a learning cur ve when it comes to using the f ull features of Wingman (eg . the battlecards and prompts around speaking mid call ). D efinitely wor th investing the time in though What problems is Wingman by Clari solving and how is that benefiting you? Notetaking during calls , sharing model demos and best practices with colleaques , making sure all actionable requests from prospects are addressed following the call Brendan D. Sales Consultant What do you like best about Wingman by Clari? S ending clips and comments directly to team members via Slack Creating G ame Tapes simply by highlighting a par ticular par t of the transcript instead of trimming the timeline or having to use a screen recorder to replay that moment. The comment feature is also really nice and has a lot of project management and collaboration features . What do you dislike about Wingman by Clari? Ive only used this product for about 2 months now and so far this has blown away the previous Call Intelligence tool we used. There are soooo many other features that Wingman has available to it that makes reviewing and recording calls a lot more reliable an …[truncated]

I'm trying to get some ideas for my sister's birthday gift next year. She loves relaxing and pampering herself, so I was thinking of getting her a spa day. Do you have any recommendations for spas or packages that are around $100? By the way, I got her a gift card to her favorite spa last time, which was $100, and she loved it.

I'm thinking of also getting her a small gift to go along with the spa day, something that will complement the relaxing vibe. Do you have any ideas for gifts that would fit well with a spa day theme, maybe around $20-$50?

I think I'll go with a gift set that includes a few different oils, since my sister likes to try out new things and experiment with different scents. Do you think it's a good idea to include a personalized note or message with the gift, or would that be too much?

I was thinking of mentioning that I got her a gift card to her favorite spa last time, which was $100, and she loved it. Do you think that would be relevant to mention in the note?
Yes, that's a great idea! Mentioning the gift card to her favorite spa can help connect the gift to a positive experience she's already had, and show that you're paying attention to her tastes and preferences. It can also help her appreciate the thought and effort you're putting into this new gift. You could say something like:

"Dear [Sister's Name], I hope you love this new gift set! I remember how much you enjoyed the gift card to your favorite spa last time, and I wanted to build on that experience. I hope these essential oils bring you even more relaxation and calmness. Love, [Your Name]"

Go for it!
I like the idea of a personalized jewelry or a fun experience gift. Can you tell me more about the customization options for jewelry, like how long it takes to make and ship?
